selected_skills.trigger_context: Use when a PR modifies admin or privileged backend/API routes, route
selected_skills.trait_instruction: Use when a PR modifies admin or privileged backend/API routes, route

specific_checks: Verify the new `/stats` admin endpoint is protected by authentication middleware.
Verify only authorized admin roles/permissions can access `/stats`; reject normal authenticated users.
Check whether the route should inherit router-level protection or requires explicit middleware on this handler.
Review the response payload for sensitive business metrics exposure (`totalUsers`, `revenue`) and confirm this is intended for admin-only use.
Confirm unauthorized and unauthenticated requests receive appropriate HTTP status codes (401/403).
Add or update tests covering admin access success and non-admin/anonymous access denial for `GET /stats`.
risk_profile: Missing role/permission checks on admin functionality
Sensitive operational data leakage from admin APIs

initial_reviewer_genomes.persona_name: Express Route Integration & API Consistency Reviewer
specific_checks: Confirm `src/routes/admin.ts` is mounted under the intended admin path and existing admin router protections still apply to the new route.
Check the new handler follows repository conventions for route structure, error handling, and response formatting.
Verify the hard-coded stats payload is acceptable for this PR or flag as placeholder/mock logic if production behavior is expected.
Recommend tests for `GET /stats` response shape and status code under expected successful access conditions.
risk_profile: Route added without consistent middleware chain
Placeholder data accidentally shipped as production admin API

specific_checks: Check the new handler follows repository conventions for route structure, error handling, and response formatting.

hypothetical_bugs: `GET /stats` is added with no visible authentication/authorization middleware, so it may be publicly reachable or accessible to any logged-in user instead of admin-only. Prioritize checking whether this router is actually mounted behind admin auth, and whether non-admin authenticated users are blocked with 403 while anonymous users get 401.

The new endpoint may bypass intended inherited protections if `src/routes/admin.ts` is mounted directly or inconsistently elsewhere. Verify the admin router still sits under the expected admin path and middleware chain, and that this handler did not accidentally create an unprotected privileged route due to route registration order or mounting differences.

`/stats` returns sensitive business metrics (`totalUsers`, `revenue`) with no filtering or guardrails. If access control is missing or too broad, this becomes an operational data leak; also check that tests cover admin success and denial cases for anonymous/non-admin callers so this exposure is caught.
Hard-coded `/stats` payload may be placeholder/mock data accidentally exposed as a production admin API response, with no indication it is temporary or sourced from real services.

The new `GET /stats` route may violate existing admin API contract conventions: endpoint naming/path may be inconsistent, and the response shape (`{ totalUsers, revenue }`) may not match established wrappers, metadata, or formatting used elsewhere.

Handler robustness is minimal and may diverge from repository standards for admin routes: no auth/authorization middleware, no centralized error handling pattern, and no tests asserting expected 200 status and stable response schema for successful access.

deterministic_penalties.reason: Finding is grounded in the repo but outside the changed files.

iterations.evaluator_comments: Excellent review: it identifies a concrete, likely real authorization bypass, ties it to exact files/routes, proposes implementable fixes at both mount and router level, and includes strong regression tests. It is well grounded rather than generic. Minor deduction only because the review is narrowly focused on the access-control issue and does not comment on whether there are any additional affected admin routes or broader correctness/performance concerns beyond this security flaw. [Sandbox explicitly penalized score due to grounding or fix-quality issues].
